intro: Nekemias grossedentata, also known as the "toothed Nekemias," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Nekemias grossedentata include essential oils, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nekemias grossedentata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including colds, coughs, and sore throats.
